observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: early_failure over-reward and no high-confidence success-like trajectories
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: partial_progress abundant but not advancing to success-like

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: persistent over-reward of early_failure trajectories and inability to generate success-like behavior
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: continued progress-conditioned refinements without component recomposition
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: early_failure remains, no success-like trajectories achieved

lesson: Progress-conditioned refinements alone are insufficient to overcome structural component deficiencies; component recomposition targeting approach_progress and terminal_reward is required to reduce early_failure over-reward and enable success-like trajectories.
future_guidance: restructure approach_progress to strongly penalize early_failure (e.g., negative bias when progress is small or speed/angle are large) to widen margin between partial_progress and early_failure
replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty (sufficiently negative) and safe_landing_bonus (stringent conditions: both legs, very low speed, small angle, sustained leg contact)
preserve progress-conditioned gating structure and components: proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout
maintain conservative mutation until clear preference pairs can be constructed
do not treat partial_progress as success_like
avoid global survival bonus without progress gating

archive_update.reason: Selected candidate is added to elite archive as the latest valid continuation, though it does not outperform the best elite candidate.

uncertainties: Low success_like count (33) in coverage but 0 in trajectory judgment summary indicates label inconsistency
No preference pairs can be constructed due to decision_level no_decision
Approach_progress component alignment uncertain due to lack of preference pairs
